repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Petrina Jackson, April 2008Evan Fay Earle, October 2009

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: Acacia was founded in 1904 by 14 Master Masons at the University of Michigan. Today it is a strong international fraternity, with Chapters at over 40 universities across the United States and one in Canada. Over 41,000 university men have called themselves Acacians, including prominent figures such as Senators, university presidents, state governors, and Nobel Laureates. Famous Acacia alumni include: President William Howard Taft, William Jennings Bryan, Cliff Hillegas (founder of Cliff's Notes), Wes Santee (former Gold Medal distance runner), and Harold Edgerton (Nobel Prize in Physics).

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes Acacia Fraternity Corporation Board of Directors minutes, correspondence, and financial reports, correspondence, alumni and membership records, fraternity rush material, constitutions and by-laws, meeting minutes, financial records, photographs, The Traveler (the alumni publication of the Acacia Fraternity, Cornell Chapter), TRIAD (the journal), building documents, architectural sketches and plans, and ledgers.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Acacia Fraternity at Cornell / Thomas J. Balcerski. Ithaca, N.Y., 2007; Acacia Fraternity Constitution and By-Laws. [S. l.], 1907; Journal of Acacia, Volumes 1-4; Wayne Heebner Rothenberger papers, 1908-1913; Thomas J. Balcerski Acacia Fraternity Papers, 2007, collection #6583; Acacia Fraternity, Cornell chapter [videorecording] : 100 years of brotherhood / director/producer, Paul Molnar. Other Acacia material may be found in the Karl M. Dallenbach papers, #2958.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Board of Directors Meeting Minutes and Reports Box 1
Series II. Chapter Operations National Box 2
Series III. Chapter Operations Local Boxes 2-4, 21, 23, 24
Series VI. Chapter Meeting Minutes (CMM) Box 8, 24
Series IX. Chapter Membership Records Box 12
Series X. Brother Membership Files Boxes 12-14
Series XI. Wing Building Documents and Architectural Sketches Box 14
Series XII. Alumni Mailing Cards, #1-854 Box 15
Series XIII. Architectural Sketches, Blueprints, and Reproductions of Deeds and Other Documents Boxes 17, 19, Mss. Collection Box 44
Series XVI. National Interfraternity Conference Minutes Box 20

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Acacia Fraternity records, #37-4-1538. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted to documented members of the fraternity (must show ID and Acacia Fraternity membership card) or by permission from either the Acacia Fraternity historian, president of the alumni board, or the National Fraternity. Additional information is provided in the accession folder.

did.unittitle: Series I. Board of Directors Meeting Minutes and Reports
Acacia Fraternity, Inc., Record of Incorporation (reproduction)
Chapter Alumni Directory and Membership Rolls

Class Lists with Addresses and Pledges
Who's Who in Acacia by Roll #

Workman's Comp. Papers and Insurance Policies

Corporation Board Agendas and Attendance records
Human Service Project – Ithaca High School
Typed Chapter Meeting Minutes-transcribed by Bradley Ayres
Chapter Meeting Minutes in book-incomplete originals

did.abstract: Brother membership files contain information about each named Acacia fraternity member. Some of the materials include correspondence with the chapter or other brothers, essays written prior to initiation about expectations for the event, and announcements of birth, marriage, and death. Alumni dues notices are included. Some of the original folders were empty and in the case of the empty folders, a list of these additional Acacia members is provided after Series XI.

did.unittitle: Series XI. Wing Building Documents and Architectural Sketches
Acacia Fraternity Specifications for Plumbing Work

did.abstract: The alumni mailing cards were originally housed in green iron drawers designed for the cards. These cards were used until the late 1970s to keep alumni mailing addresses up to date. Generally, they are organized chronologically by initiation date.

did.unittitle: Alumni directory index card set with charter number
Series XIII. Architectural Sketches, Blueprints, and Reproductions of Deeds and Other Documents
Sketches and Blueprints (Alterations, Additions, Preliminary Plans, Dream House)
Harriet H. Williams Proposed Apartment Renovations and Acacia Dream House Student Room, Sketch by AJ
Reproduction of 1923-2005 Sketches and Blueprints (Alterations, Additions, Preliminary Plans, Dream House)
Reproduction of Harriet H. Williams Proposed Apartment Renovations and Acacia Dream House Student Room, Sketch by AJ
Reproduction of deeds related to 318 Highland Road
Reproduction of Constitution and By-Laws, October 1921
Page from 1909 Cornellian with Acacia Fraternity Crest
Reproduction of Postcards of Ithaca and Cornell

did.abstract: Various printed materials related to the New York Council of Deliberation, Ancient Accepted Scottish Rite Northern Masonic Jurisdiction. These materials were collected by members of the Cornell Chapter of Acacia Fraternity during the years of its formal affiliation with Masonry, 1907 to 1933. These materials survived the various moves of the chapter and were stored in the chapter library. In February 2012, as part of a complete renovation of the library, the decision was made by Thomas Balcerski and Steve Stein to relocate these materials to the Acacia Fraternity Records held by the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections. It is hoped these materials will be of interest to researchers of Masonic history and especially the history of Masons in New York State.

did.unittitle: 1908 Proceedings of the Council of Deliberation, State of New York, Ancient Accepted Scottish Rite Northern Masonic Jurisdiction. Buffalo, New York.
1910 Proceedings of the Council of Deliberation, State of New York, Ancient Accepted Scottish Rite Northern Masonic Jurisdiction. Corning, New York.
1911 Proceedings of the Council of Deliberation, State of New York, Ancient Accepted Scottish Rite Northern Masonic Jurisdiction. Rochester, New York.
1912 Proceedings of the Council of Deliberation, State of New York, Ancient Accepted Scottish Rite Northern Masonic Jurisdiction. Niagara Falls, New York.
1915 Proceedings of the Council of Deliberation, State of New York, Ancient Accepted Scottish Rite Northern Masonic Jurisdiction. Brooklyn, New York.
1916 Proceedings of the Council of Deliberation, State of New York, Ancient Accepted Scottish Rite Northern Masonic Jurisdiction. Olean, New York.

Proceedings of the Council of Deliberation, State of New York, Ancient Accepted Scottish Rite Northern Masonic Jurisdiction. Syracuse, New York. October 29, 1917. With Supplement by G. Alfred Lawrence, 32o, M.D., Ph.D., Formerly Grand Surgeon, N.Y. Council of Deliberation. List of Officers of the Bodies of the Ancient Accepted Scottish Rite in the State of New York, 1867—1917.

Proceedings of the Fifty-Eighth Council of Deliberation, State of New York, Ancient Accepted Scottish Rite Northern Masonic Jurisdiction. Utica, New York. Saturday, July 30, 1927. With Supplement by G. Alfred Lawrence, 32o, M.D., Ph.D., Formerly Grand Surgeon, N.Y. Council of Deliberation. List of Officers of the Bodies of the Ancient Accepted Scottish Rite in the State of New York, 1867—1927.
Proceedings of the Grand Lodge of Free and Accepted Masons of the State of New York. One hundred and fiftieth annual communication.

List of Members of the Supreme Council of Sovereign Grand Inspectors-General of the Thirty-Third and Last Degree, Ancient Accepted Scottish Rite for the Northern Masonic Jurisdiction of the United States of America; Officers of the Council of Deliberation and Officers of the Various Subordinate Scottish Rite Bodies of the State of New York to December 31, 1917, by G. Alfred Lawrence, 32o, M.D., Ph.D., Formerly Grand Surgeon, N.Y. Council of Deliberation.

List of Members of the Supreme Council of Sovereign Grand Inspectors-General of the Thirty-Third and Last Degree, Ancient Accepted Scottish Rite for the Northern Masonic Jurisdiction of the United States of America; Officers of the Council of Deliberation and Officers of the Various Subordinate Scottish Rite Bodies of the State of New York to December 31, 1927, by G. Alfred Lawrence, 32o, M.D., Ph.D., Formerly Grand Surgeon, N.Y. Council of Deliberation.

Proceedings of the Supreme Council of Sovereign Grand Inspectors-General of the Thirty-Third and Last Degree, Ancient Accepted Scottish Rite for the Northern Masonic Jurisdiction of the United States of America from March 21, 1847, to May 20, 1861, inclusive. Edited by G. Alfred Lawrence, o M.D., Ph.D., of New York.
The Masonry Glossary. International Masonry Institute
Series XVI. National Interfraternity Conference Minutes
